scenario_title: Delete then add the same RRset in one UPDATE
test_rationale: This test creates one RRset, then issues a single UPDATE that deletes that RRset and immediately adds a new TXT RRset at the same owner name. That shape directly exercises the RFC 2136 gap around conflicting operations in one message: a server may execute the operations in order and end with the new TXT record, or it may reject the message or preserve the original state. The follow-up QUERY makes the final zone content observable so the runner can distinguish sequential processing from rejection or normalization behavior.

group_1.behavior: Processes the UPDATE sequentially, so the delete_rrset removes the old TXT RRset and the subsequent add_rr installs the new TXT RRset.

group_2.behavior: Rejects or otherwise does not apply the conflicting same-message delete-then-add sequence as a valid sequential update.
group_2.post_state: hold.campus.edu. TXT remains "old" or the RRset is unchanged.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2 and §3.4.3 define delete and add semantics individually, but do not specify a canonical intra-message ordering or conflict-resolution rule when both operations target the same owner/type within one UPDATE. Implementations may therefore apply the operations sequentially, normalize them as a net delta, or reject the message, producing observable differences in reply code and/or final RRset contents.
